GREAT ONE FOR THE RAILROADS

pages.raw_text:
far outstripped all other roads Early
in the year it sold $75000,000 of new
stock for $50. 100000. This was fol-
lowed by the sale of over 100000, 900
of stocks and bonds on the subsidiary
properties. This heavy borrowing by
Pennsylvania absorbed most Of the
floating supply of capital and the
other railroads later in the year found
great difficulty in borrowing money.
The congestion of securities was 80
great that the other railroads were
unable to sell their bonds except at a
heavy discount. Most of the COM
panies were unwilling to do this, and
instead borrowed on their notes run
ming from one to two years. In this
way the Burlington, the Union Paci-
fic, the Great Northern, the st. Louis
& San Francisco and the Lake Shore
borrowed from $4,000. 900 to $4,000
900 apiece. Owing to the unfavorable
monetary conditions all of the rail-
roads that could postponed their Dor
rowing until the money market shall
have become easier.
